subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Post-Storm Hidden Dangers
description: Learn Post-Storm Hidden Dangers in one minute.

hook.text: More people die after storms than during them. Downed power lines, contaminated water, and carbon monoxide.

buildup.text: Flooded areas hide downed electrical lines—water conducts electricity across entire surfaces.

discovery.text: Generators run indoors kill dozens per year from carbon monoxide—an invisible, odorless gas.

twist.text: Chainsaw injuries spike after storms as untrained homeowners clear fallen trees themselves.

climax.text: Treating the aftermath as still dangerous—boiling water, avoiding flood zones—saves lives.

punchline.text: Storm passed. Danger didn't. Power lines, bad water, and exhaust still kill.

quiz.question: What is the most common hidden danger after major storms?
options: Downed power lines, contaminated water, and carbon monoxide from generators
